description: Fix the recruit mandate bug (allowing 2 figures from same fortress) and remove the 'Abrir Mercado' button during Train mandate, and format Marshal instructions as numbered list
steps: 1. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, fix the recruitPlaceFigure function: Add state tracking for which fortresses have already been used for placement during this player's recruit turn. When a Bushi or Monster is placed in a province, mark that fortress as 'used'. A subsequent Bushi/Monster placement must go to a DIFFERENT fortress. The bonus placement (for issuer/ally) can go to ANY province the player has a fortress in, regardless of whether that fortress was already used. When a Shinto is placed (to a temple), it does NOT consume a specific fortress slot - it just decrements a counter from the available total. This means if a player has 2 fortresses and places a Shinto first, they can still place a Bushi/Monster at either fortress afterward.

2. Add a new field to GameState in src/types/game.ts: `recruitUsedFortressProvinces: string[]` to track which fortress provinces have been used for non-Shinto placements during the current player's recruit turn. Reset this array when advancing to the next player in advanceRecruitResolution.

3. Modify recruitPlaceFigure in src/utils/gameLogic.ts: For bushi/monster placements, check that the province is not already in recruitUsedFortressProvinces (unless it is a bonus placement and has multiple fortresses). After a successful non-Shinto placement, add the provinceId to recruitUsedFortressProvinces.
4. In the store (gameStore.ts), when placing a Shinto via doRecruitPlaceTempleShinto, do NOT add any province to the used list since Shinto goes to temples regardless of fortress location.

5. In src/components/ActionPanel.tsx: Remove the 'Abrir Mercado' / 'Open Market' button that appears during Train mandate (the setShowTrainModal(true) button). The Season Cards modal is already auto-opened by PoliticsTrack's useEffect when trainMandateActive becomes true, so the button is redundant.

6. In src/components/ActionPanel.tsx: Format the marshal move instruction text (actions.marshalMoveSteps) properly. Currently it renders as a single

element. Change it to render each step on its own line (use a numbered list or line breaks). Do the same for the marshalMoveInstruction text.
acceptance_criteria: Build passes with npm run build
The recruitPlaceFigure function in gameLogic.ts enforces: only 1 non-Shinto figure per fortress province per recruit turn
Shinto placements do not consume fortress province slots
Bonus placement (issuer/ally extra) can go to any fortress province regardless of usage
The 'Abrir Mercado' / 'Open Market' button no longer appears in the Train mandate section of ActionPanel
Marshal instructions display as a numbered list, not a single block of text
findings: Build passes successfully. The recruit fix uses the usedFortressProvinces array to track which fortress provinces have been used for base (non-Shinto) placements. The bonus placement (for issuer/ally) is allowed at any fortress province. Shinto placements never add to the used list. The array is reset when advancing to the next player in recruit resolution order. The setShowTrainModal import was removed from ActionPanel since the button was deleted.
